text:
## Overview
All Mines system access is provisioned through the IT Service Desk based on your role (student, faculty, staff, researcher). Some systems require explicit approval from a supervisor or data steward.

### New student account activation
- MultiPass accounts are created automatically after enrollment confirmation.
- Activation takes 24–48 hours after the Registrar confirms enrollment.
- Activation email is sent to your personal email on file with Admissions.
- If you have not received an activation email within 48 hours, contact the IT Service Desk.

### New employee account setup
1. HR submits a provisioning request when your hire paperwork is complete.
2. Your MultiPass account is created 1–2 business days before your start date.
3. On your start date, activate at myaccount.mines.edu using the temporary password from your activation email.
4. Complete Duo MFA enrollment immediately after activation.
5. Standard access includes: email, Canvas, VPN, OneDrive. Banner, Workday, and Edify require separate role provisioning.

### Requesting access to a specific system
1. Log in to the IT Help Center at helpcenter.mines.edu.
2. Submit a "Service Request" → "Access Request."
3. Include: system name, required access level, business justification, supervisor name.
4. Your supervisor will receive an approval email — access is provisioned after their approval.
5. Processing time: 1–3 business days after supervisor approval.

### Requesting elevated or admin access
- Elevated access (admin rights, data domain access, reporting access) requires:
  - Supervisor approval
  - Data steward approval (for sensitive data systems like Edify and Banner)
  - IT Security review for admin-level permissions
- Processing time: 3–5 business days.

### Access for temporary or contract staff
- Submit a "Temporary Access Request" — specify the exact access end date.
- Temporary accounts are auto-disabled on the specified end date.
- Extensions must be requested before the expiry date.

### Transferring to a new role/department
- Submit an "Access Transfer Request" with your new supervisor's approval.
- Old role access is revoked; new role access is granted.
- Some systems require your old department to formally release the access.

### Reporting unauthorized access
- If you believe someone has unauthorized access to your account or data, contact IT Security immediately: security@mines.edu or 303-278-4357 (select Security option).

## When to Escalate
- Account not created 48 hours after enrollment or hire date.
- Supervisor approval sent but access not provisioned after 3 business days.
- Urgent access needed for time-sensitive work — call IT Service Desk directly.

## Overview
Banner is Colorado School of Mines' student information system, used for course registration, academic records, financial aid, and student accounts. Access is via Single Sign-On (SSO) using your MultiPass credentials.

### Cannot log in to Banner
1. Verify you are using your full Mines email (username@mines.edu) as your login.
2. Confirm your MultiPass password is correct — test at myaccount.mines.edu.
3. Clear browser cookies and cache, then retry.
4. Try a different browser (Chrome or Firefox recommended).
5. Disable browser extensions that may block SSO redirects.

### Account locked after failed attempts
- MultiPass accounts lock after 5 failed login attempts.
- Self-unlock at myaccount.mines.edu → "Unlock Account."
- If unlock fails, contact IT Service Desk: 303-278-4357.

### SSO redirect loop or "Access Denied" error
- Clear all cookies for mines.edu and banner.mines.edu.
- Log out of all Google/Microsoft sessions in that browser.
- If error persists, your Banner role may not be provisioned — submit an access request through the IT portal.

### New student: account not yet active
- Banner accounts activate 48–72 hours after enrollment confirmation.
- Check activation status at myaccount.mines.edu.
- If more than 72 hours have passed, contact the Registrar's Office.

### Two-factor authentication (Duo) prompt not appearing
- Ensure your Duo device is enrolled at duo.mines.edu.
- Check your mobile device has Duo Mobile installed and push notifications enabled.
- See the MFA/Duo knowledge base article for full troubleshooting.

## When to Escalate
- Account locked and self-service unlock fails.
- SSO errors persist after clearing cache and trying alternate browser.
- Role access is missing after more than 3 business days of enrollment.
- Banner shows incorrect student or financial data.

## Overview
Canvas is Mines' Learning Management System (LMS). Used for course materials, assignments, grades, and communication. Access at canvas.mines.edu using MultiPass credentials.

### Grades not syncing from Canvas to Banner
- Canvas grade sync to Banner runs nightly at 2:00 AM MST.
- If grades are missing after 24 hours, the instructor must manually trigger the sync.
- Students: contact your instructor first — they initiate the sync, not IT.
- Instructors: go to Grades → Sync → Export to Banner. If the sync button is grayed out, contact IT.

### Cannot access a course in Canvas
1. Confirm the instructor has published the course (unpublished courses are invisible to students).
2. Verify your enrollment in Banner — Canvas enrollment mirrors Banner registration.
3. If enrolled in Banner but missing in Canvas, wait 2–4 hours for the sync to run.
4. If still missing after 4 hours, submit a ticket with your course name and CRN.

### Assignment submission errors
- Check file size: Canvas limit is 500MB per file.
- Supported formats depend on assignment settings — check the assignment instructions.
- If submit button is missing, the deadline may have passed or the assignment requires a different submission type (media, text, etc.).

### Canvas mobile app not loading
- Force close and reopen the app.
- Log out and log back in using your mines.edu credentials.
- Ensure your app is updated to the latest version.

### Video content not playing in Canvas
- Videos hosted in Kaltura or YouTube require a stable internet connection.
- Disable browser ad-blockers or VPN that may block media domains.
- Try downloading the video if a download link is available.

### Missing quiz or exam in Canvas
- The instructor may have set an availability window — check the course calendar.
- Contact your instructor to confirm the quiz is published and available to your section.

## When to Escalate
- Grade sync failures persist more than 48 hours after instructor action.
- Course enrollment discrepancy between Banner and Canvas after 4 hours.
- Canvas system-wide outage — check status.instructure.com.

## Overview
Edify is Mines' institutional data and analytics platform. It provides access to reporting, dashboards, and data pipelines for institutional research and administrative analytics.

### Cannot access Edify / "Permission Denied"
1. Edify access requires explicit role provisioning — it is not granted by default to all staff.
2. Request access through the IT portal: submit an Access Request ticket with your department and required data domains.
3. Your supervisor or data steward must approve the request before IT provisions access.

### Dashboard not loading or returning no data
1. Refresh the page and clear browser cache.
2. Check your date filters — default views sometimes default to a range with no data.
3. Verify you have the correct data domain access for the report you are viewing.
4. Check if the underlying data pipeline has completed — pipelines run on a scheduled basis (typically nightly).

### Data pipeline errors / stale data
- Most Edify pipelines run nightly between 1:00–4:00 AM MST.
- Data visible before 6:00 AM may be from the previous day's run.
- If data appears more than 24 hours stale, the pipeline may have failed.
- Pipeline failure escalation: submit a ticket with the specific report name and the expected vs actual data date.

### Export failing or timing out
- Large exports (>50,000 rows) may time out in the browser interface.
- Use the scheduled export feature to queue large exports — results are emailed when ready.
- If scheduled export also fails, reduce the date range and export in segments.

### Incorrect data in reports
- First verify the filter settings on the report — many apparent data errors are filter misconfigurations.
- If data is genuinely wrong, document the specific record or metric and expected value.
- Submit a ticket with: report name, incorrect value, expected value, and a screenshot.

## When to Escalate
- Access request not fulfilled after 3 business days.
- Pipeline shows failure status in the Edify admin panel.
- Data integrity issues confirmed after filter verification.
- System-wide Edify unavailability.

## Overview
Mines provides Microsoft Exchange email to all students, faculty, and staff. Your email address is username@mines.edu. Access via Outlook desktop, web (outlook.office.com), or the Outlook mobile app.

### Cannot log in to email / Outlook Web
1. Go to outlook.office.com and sign in with your full mines.edu address.
2. If redirected to a login error, reset your MultiPass password (see Password Reset article).
3. Clear browser cookies for microsoft.com and outlook.com, then retry.
4. Complete Duo MFA if prompted — email requires MFA for web access.

### Outlook desktop app not syncing
1. Check your internet connection.
2. In Outlook, look for "Disconnected" or "Trying to connect" in the status bar.
3. File → Office Account → Sign Out, then Sign In again with your mines.edu account.
4. If Outlook prompts for a password in a loop: open Credential Manager (Windows) → remove all Microsoft/Office credentials → restart Outlook.

### Not receiving emails
1. Check your Junk/Spam folder — Outlook's spam filter sometimes misclassifies legitimate emails.
2. Check your inbox rules: Settings → View all Outlook settings → Rules — a rule may be redirecting or deleting messages.
3. Check if your mailbox is full: Settings → General → Storage. Mines provides 50GB per mailbox.
4. If expecting a specific email, ask the sender to resend and check for delivery failure notices on their end.

### Email marked as spam or bouncing
- Mines' email security scans all outbound messages. Bulk email from your mines.edu account may be flagged.
- If you need to send bulk announcements, use the approved mailing list service through IT.
- For legitimate emails being blocked by recipients: submit a ticket with the recipient domain for IT to investigate.

### Setting up email on mobile
1. Install Outlook mobile (iOS/Android).
2. Add account → use your mines.edu address.
3. You will be redirected to Mines SSO — sign in with MultiPass.
4. Complete Duo MFA enrollment if prompted.
5. Do not use the native iOS/Android mail app with Exchange — it does not support Mines' MFA requirements.

### Email quota full
- Default mailbox size: 50GB.
- Archive old emails: File → Cleanup Tools → Archive (Outlook desktop).
- Delete large attachments: Sort inbox by size, delete emails with large attachments you no longer need.
- Empty Deleted Items and Junk folders regularly.

## When to Escalate
- Cannot access email after password reset.
- Mailbox appears empty (possible data loss).
- Receiving excessive spam that spam filters are not catching.
- Need a shared mailbox or distribution list set up for a department.

## Overview
MESA (Mines Enterprise Support & Advising Agent) provides AI-assisted first-line support. When an issue cannot be resolved through self-service or AI guidance, it is escalated to the IT Service Desk.

- **Phone**: 303-278-HELP (4357) — Monday–Friday, 7:30 AM–5:30 PM MST
- **Walk-in**: Coolbaugh Hall, Room 209 — Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM–5:00 PM MST
- **Help Center**: helpcenter.mines.edu — submit tickets and browse the knowledge base 24/7
- **Emergency after-hours**: 303-278-4357 (select Emergency option)

Contact IT Service Desk immediately (do not wait) for:
- Suspected account compromise or unauthorized access
- Data breach or accidental exposure of sensitive data
- Ransomware or malware infection on any device
- Complete system or network outage affecting multiple users
- Loss of access to systems needed for time-sensitive academic or research work

Your ticket will be escalated to an IT staff member if:
- The AI-provided resolution does not resolve your issue after attempting the steps
- The issue requires admin-level access or system-side configuration
- The issue affects multiple users (infrastructure problem)
- Sensitive data is involved that requires a specialist
- The issue has persisted more than 2 business days without resolution

| Priority | Description | Target Response |
|---|---|---|
| Critical | System outage, security incident | Within 1 hour |
| High | Single-user complete loss of access to critical system | Within 4 hours |
| Medium | Partial access issue, workaround available | Within 1 business day |
| Low | General inquiry, non-blocking issue | Within 3 business days |

To speed up resolution when talking to IT staff, have ready:
1. Your Mines username (not your full email)
2. The system you are trying to access (Banner, Canvas, Workday, etc.)
3. The exact error message (screenshot if possible)
4. When the problem started
5. What you have already tried
6. Your device type and operating system

## Privacy Reminder
When submitting support tickets or communicating with IT, do not include:
- Your full Social Security Number
- Your complete MultiPass password
- Financial account numbers
- Any other sensitive personal information
If IT needs to verify your identity, they will do so through official channels (Mines ID number, not SSN).

## Overview
Mines uses Duo Security for multi-factor authentication (MFA) on all systems accessed via MultiPass SSO. MFA adds a second verification step beyond your password.

### Enrolling in Duo for the first time
1. Go to duo.mines.edu.
2. Sign in with your MultiPass credentials.
3. Follow the enrollment wizard — choose your preferred authentication method:
   - **Duo Mobile app** (recommended): install on iOS or Android, scan QR code.
   - **SMS passcode**: receive a text message with a code.
   - **Hardware token**: available from IT Service Desk for users without smartphones.

### Adding a new device
1. Log in to duo.mines.edu → Manage Devices → Add a device.
2. Follow the same enrollment steps for the new device.
3. Keep your old device active until the new one is confirmed working.

### Duo push notification not arriving
1. Check your phone has internet or cellular connectivity.
2. Open the Duo Mobile app and tap "Get a Duo Push" manually.
3. Check notification settings: Settings → Notifications → Duo Mobile → Allow Notifications (on).
4. If push does not work, use "Enter a Passcode" — open the Duo Mobile app to generate a 6-digit code.

### Lost or replaced phone
1. If you have a second device enrolled: use it to log in.
2. If you have no other device: call the IT Service Desk at 303-278-4357 to get a temporary bypass code. Have your Mines ID ready.
3. After getting a bypass code, immediately enroll your new device at duo.mines.edu.

### Duo says "Error: Access Denied"
- Your account may be restricted from MFA bypass. Contact IT Service Desk.
- If you are traveling internationally, Duo push may fail — use passcodes from the app instead (works offline).

### "Invalid device" error after phone upgrade
- Old device enrollment must be removed. Log in from a computer using a passcode or bypass code, then go to duo.mines.edu → Manage Devices → Remove old device.

### Duo prompting too frequently
- When logging in, check "Remember me for X days" to reduce prompts on trusted devices.
- If you're prompted every session despite "remember me," check if your browser blocks cookies for mines.edu.

## When to Escalate
- Cannot log in with any Duo method and need immediate system access.
- Bypass code from IT does not work.
- Account shows locked in Duo admin — requires IT admin intervention.

## Overview
Mines provides campus Wi-Fi, wired Ethernet in buildings, and GlobalProtect VPN for secure off-campus access to internal systems.

### Connecting to Mines Wi-Fi
- Primary network: **MinesAir** — requires MultiPass login. Use for all campus computing.
- Guest network: **MinesGuest** — for visitors, no Mines account required. Limited to basic internet.
- eduroam: available for visitors from partner institutions — use their home institution credentials.

### Cannot connect to MinesAir
1. Forget the MinesAir network on your device and reconnect.
2. Ensure you are entering your full mines.edu email as the username.
3. Accept the security certificate if prompted.
4. On Windows: go to Network Settings → Manage Known Networks → Remove MinesAir → reconnect.
5. On Mac: System Preferences → Network → Wi-Fi → Advanced → remove MinesAir → reconnect.
6. If still failing, register your device's MAC address at network.mines.edu.

### Slow Wi-Fi or frequent disconnections
- Avoid connecting in high-density areas during peak hours (10 AM–2 PM in lecture halls).
- Use 5GHz band when available (shows as MinesAir-5G on some access points).
- Wired Ethernet is available in all classrooms, offices, and the library — always faster and more stable.

### Installing GlobalProtect VPN
1. Download the GlobalProtect client from vpn.mines.edu.
2. Install and launch GlobalProtect.
3. Enter portal address: `vpn.mines.edu`.
4. Sign in with your MultiPass credentials and complete Duo MFA.

### VPN not connecting
1. Ensure you are not already on the campus network — VPN is for off-campus use.
2. Check your internet connection works without VPN.
3. Disconnect and reconnect GlobalProtect.
4. Restart the GlobalProtect service: on Windows, open Services → find "PanGPS" → Restart.
5. If "Authentication failed": reset your MultiPass password (see Password Reset article).

### Which systems require VPN
- Edify analytics platform (off-campus access)
- Internal department file shares
- Some administrative applications
- Banner and Canvas do NOT require VPN — accessible directly from the internet.

### Split tunneling
- GlobalProtect uses split tunneling by default: only Mines-internal traffic routes through VPN, regular internet traffic goes direct.
- If you notice all internet traffic is slow on VPN, contact IT — this may indicate a misconfiguration.

## When to Escalate
- Entire building has no network access (infrastructure issue).
- VPN installation fails or client crashes repeatedly.
- MAC address registration required but not resolving connection.

## Overview
All Mines students, faculty, and staff have OneDrive for Business storage as part of the Microsoft 365 subscription. OneDrive is accessed through the Microsoft 365 portal at office.com or via the desktop sync client.

### OneDrive not syncing on desktop
1. Check the OneDrive icon in the system tray — a red X indicates a sync error; click for details.
2. Common causes: file name contains invalid characters (: * ? < > | "), file path is too long (>260 characters on Windows), or file is locked by another application.
3. Pause and resume sync: right-click OneDrive tray icon → Pause syncing → Resume.
4. Sign out and sign back in: OneDrive tray → Settings → Account → Sign out, then sign in with your mines.edu account.
5. Reset OneDrive client: press Win+R, run `%localappdata%\Microsoft\OneDrive\onedrive.exe /reset`.

### Files missing from OneDrive
- Check the Recycle Bin on OneDrive web (office.com → OneDrive → Recycle Bin). Files are retained for 93 days.
- If shared files are missing, the owner may have revoked access or deleted them.
- Check "Shared with me" section for files shared by others.

### Cannot upload — "Storage quota exceeded"
- Mines provides 1TB of OneDrive storage per user.
- Check your storage usage: OneDrive web → Settings → Storage.
- Delete or move old files to free space.
- Contact IT if you need a quota increase for research or department purposes.

### Sharing permissions not working
- External sharing (outside mines.edu) may be restricted by IT policy.
- Internal sharing: use "Share" → enter the person's mines.edu email.
- If "Anyone with the link" option is unavailable, your account is restricted to internal sharing only.

### OneDrive files not accessible offline
- Enable offline access: right-click the folder in File Explorer → Always keep on this device.
- Ensure OneDrive sync client is installed and running.

### Sync conflicts
- OneDrive creates a conflict copy when the same file is edited on multiple devices simultaneously.
- Look for files named "filename (User's conflicted copy)".
- Manually compare and merge the versions, then delete the conflict copy.

## When to Escalate
- Sync errors persist after reset and re-sign-in.
- Large volumes of files missing that are not in the Recycle Bin.
- Storage quota issues that require admin override.

## Overview
MultiPass is Mines' identity and access management system. Your MultiPass username and password are used to log in to Banner, Canvas, Workday, email, VPN, and all other campus systems.

### Reset your password online
1. Go to myaccount.mines.edu.
2. Click "Forgot Password" or "Reset Password."
3. Enter your mines.edu email address.
4. You will receive a verification code via your registered recovery email or phone number.
5. Enter the code and set a new password.
6. Password requirements: minimum 12 characters, at least one uppercase, one lowercase, one number, one special character.

### Unlock a locked account
1. Go to myaccount.mines.edu.
2. Click "Unlock Account."
3. Verify your identity via recovery method.
4. Your account unlocks immediately.

### Password expired
- MultiPass passwords expire every 365 days.
- You will receive email warnings at 30, 14, and 7 days before expiry.
- Expired passwords can be reset at myaccount.mines.edu even after expiry.

### Forgot recovery email or phone
- If you cannot verify your identity through self-service, bring your Mines ID to the IT Service Desk in person.
- Location: Coolbaugh Hall, Room 209. Hours: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM–5:00 PM.
- Remote options: call 303-278-4357 with your student/employee ID number for identity verification.

### After resetting password
- Wait 5–10 minutes for the new password to propagate across all Mines systems.
- Update saved passwords in your browser and mobile devices.
- Update password in the OneDrive sync client and any email clients (Outlook desktop, mobile).
- If using GlobalProtect VPN, disconnect and reconnect with the new password.

### Setting up password recovery methods
1. Log in to myaccount.mines.edu.
2. Go to Security Info → Add method.
3. Add at least one recovery method: email (personal, non-mines) or phone number.
4. Keeping two recovery methods ensures you can always reset your own password.

## When to Escalate
- Self-service reset fails after multiple attempts.
- Cannot verify identity via any recovery method.
- Account shows as "disabled" rather than "locked" — requires admin intervention.

## Overview
Mines provides free or discounted software to students, faculty, and staff through institutional licenses. Software is available through the Mines Software Portal at software.mines.edu.

### Microsoft 365 (Office Suite)
- All students, faculty, and staff receive Microsoft 365 for free.
- Includes: Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Teams, OneDrive, Outlook.
- Install: office.com → sign in with mines.edu → Install Office.
- Up to 5 devices (PC, Mac, tablet, phone).

### MATLAB
- Free for all currently enrolled students and active faculty/staff.
- Download from software.mines.edu → MATLAB.
- Use your mines.edu email to create a MathWorks account and link to the campus license.
- If license shows as expired, your MathWorks account may need to be re-linked — contact IT.

### Adobe Creative Cloud
- Available to specific departments and programs — not a campus-wide license.
- Contact your department administrator or IT to check eligibility.
- Students in Engineering, Computer Science, and Design programs typically have access.

### ESRI ArcGIS
- Available for Geology, Civil Engineering, and related programs.
- Request access through the IT portal with your faculty supervisor's approval.

### Antivirus — Crowdstrike Falcon
- Free for all Mines devices (personal and university-owned).
- Download from software.mines.edu → Security → Crowdstrike Falcon.
- Required on any device that accesses Mines network resources remotely.

### Software shows license expired
1. Sign out of the application and sign back in with your mines.edu credentials.
2. Ensure your Mines enrollment or employment is still active.
3. For Microsoft 365: go to office.com → Account → Subscription — should show active.
4. If license expired mid-term for active students: submit a ticket — IT will re-link your account.

### Cannot find software in the portal
- The software portal lists currently licensed titles. If a title is missing, Mines may not have a license.
- Submit a software request through the IT portal if your course requires software not in the portal.
- Faculty can request evaluation licenses for academic use.

### Installation fails
- Ensure you have admin rights on your device for installation.
- On Mines-managed lab computers, software must be installed by IT — submit a ticket.
- Check minimum system requirements for the software version.

## When to Escalate
- License verification fails after sign-out/sign-in.
- Software required for a class is unavailable.
- Installation fails on a Mines-managed device.

## Overview
Workday is Mines' HR and payroll system for faculty, staff, and student employees. Access at workday.mines.edu using MultiPass credentials.

### Cannot see pay stubs or payroll information
1. Verify you are signed in with your mines.edu account (not a personal email).
2. In Workday, navigate to: Pay → Pay Slips. If this menu is missing, your worker profile may not have payroll access.
3. New employees: payroll visibility is granted after your first pay period is processed (typically 2–4 weeks after hire date).
4. If you are a student employee, ensure your student employment contract is active in Workday.

### Direct deposit not set up or incorrect
- Navigate to: Pay → Payment Elections to add or update your bank account.
- Changes take effect on the next payroll cycle (changes submitted after the payroll cutoff apply to the following period).
- Payroll cutoff dates are posted on the HR calendar at hr.mines.edu.

### Paycheck amount appears incorrect
- Review your pay slip details: gross pay, deductions, tax withholdings.
- Check if a benefit deduction changed — open enrollment changes take effect at the start of the new benefit year.
- If a deduction seems wrong, contact HR directly: hr@mines.edu or 303-273-3250.
- Do not attempt to resolve payroll discrepancies through IT — these require HR and Payroll team involvement.

### Cannot submit timesheet
- Timesheet submission windows open/close on a schedule — check the HR calendar.
- If the "Submit" button is missing, your manager may not have approved your time entry setup.
- Bi-weekly employees must submit by Sunday 11:59 PM of the pay period end.

### Missing benefit elections in Workday
- Benefits are visible under: Benefits → Benefits → Benefit Elections.
- New hire benefit enrollment window is 30 days from hire date. After that, changes require a qualifying life event.
- Contact HR Benefits Office for questions on specific benefit plans.

### Workday login issues
- Workday uses MultiPass SSO — see the Banner Authentication article for password reset steps.
- If you see "You are not authorized," your Workday role has not been provisioned. Contact HR.

## When to Escalate
- Payroll amounts are incorrect — escalate to HR/Payroll, not IT.
- Workday access not provisioned more than 3 business days after hire.
- Timesheet submission failures that persist after browser refresh.
